scenarios.description: Compare deployment configurations across two clusters to find discrepancies.
scenarios.final_response: Compared deployment 'api-gateway' across prod-east and prod-west clusters. Found replica count mismatch (3 vs 5) and image tag drift (v2.1.0 vs v2.0.8) because prod-west missed the last rollout. Recommend syncing via GitOps to align both clusters.

scenarios.description: Aggregate health scores and alert counts across all fleet clusters.
scenarios.final_response: Fleet health summary across 4 clusters: prod-east healthy (0 firing alerts), prod-west degraded (3 alerts, 2 crashlooping pods), staging healthy, dev has 1 warning. Overall fleet health is 75% because prod-west has unresolved incidents requiring attention.

scenarios.description: Detect configuration drift between clusters for a critical workload.
scenarios.final_response: Configuration drift detected in 'payment-service' deployment. Prod-east has env var DATABASE_POOL_SIZE=20 while prod-west has 10 because a manual patch was applied to prod-east outside GitOps. Recommend reverting the manual change and updating the Git source of truth.

scenarios.description: Safe node maintenance sequence: cordon, drain, verify workload redistribution.
scenarios.final_response: Node worker-3 was safely cordoned and drained for maintenance. Verified PDB constraints were respected and all evicted pods rescheduled to worker-1 and worker-2 because sufficient capacity existed. Node is ready for patching. Run uncordon after maintenance.

scenarios.description: Audit TLS certificate expiry across fleet clusters.
scenarios.final_response: Certificate audit across fleet found 2 certificates expiring within 30 days: ingress wildcard cert on prod-east (12 days) and API serving cert on prod-west (28 days) because automatic renewal was not configured. Recommend enabling cert-manager auto-renewal and immediate manual renewal for the 12-day cert.

scenarios.description: Compare CPU usage across all ACM managed clusters using Thanos.
scenarios.final_response: CPU usage comparison across 4 managed clusters: prod-east at 72% utilization, prod-west at 45%, staging at 30%, dev at 15%. Prod-east is approaching capacity because workload growth has been steady.

scenarios.description: Query memory usage for a specific managed cluster via ACM Thanos.
scenarios.final_response: Memory usage for cluster prod-east over the past hour: 85% utilized (68Gi of 80Gi). Top consumers are namespace payments (22Gi) and api-gateway (15Gi).

scenarios.description: Check API server latency and error rates across all ACM clusters.
scenarios.final_response: API server health across fleet: all clusters under 200ms p99 latency except prod-west at 850ms with elevated 5xx rate. Root cause is etcd latency because the etcd disk is at 90% capacity.

scenarios.description: Agent handles Thanos-incompatible PromQL gracefully with warning and alternative.
scenarios.final_response: The original query using group_left is not compatible with ACM Thanos. Rewrote as two separate queries for node CPU and node metadata. Node-3 has highest CPU at 92%.

scenarios.description: Monitor etcd health across all managed clusters using ACM fleet recipes.
scenarios.final_response: etcd health across fleet: all clusters have leaders, db sizes range 120MB-450MB. Prod-west has 3 leader changes in the last hour because of network instability.

description: Multi-cluster operations — cross-cluster comparison, health summary, drift detection, cordon/drain, certificate audit, ACM Thanos metrics
